raw_consumer_statement:
I am reopening case XXXX I did not make the purchase in question. I have local law enforcement involved, the police report is attached, and i am working with the merchant. 

I can not believe what an absurd situation this has become. CITI has failed to protect me from fraud according to the regulations in place from the federal government. 

1. I received a call from CITI to alert me that fraud happened. I responded in real time and they proceeded with next steps to close my account and reissue my card.

2. I confirmed that I had my card AND I WAS NOT AT THE LOCATION where the card was used. I was miles away.

3. I did not give anyone else permission to use my card. 

And to update my case, I did time researching to learn that XXXX XXXX can be cloned. The basis for closing my claim without issuing a credit was the claim that this is not possible. 
This would explain why the card was with me and I was not at the location. CITI has hyper focused on the assumption that my chip was used while ignoring the fact that I could not have physically engaged in the transaction since I was not there. 
Cloning XXXXXXXX XXXX is not only possible but it is becoming increasingly common. It's called credit card cloning and card skimming. This includes a lower technological method that involves copying credit card chips to the magnetic strip so that the reader can not distinguish whether the XXXXXXXX XXXX was used. 
I am requesting evidence from the credit card issuer that they used to make this determination. If they are ignoring the indisputable fact that XXXX cloning is an increasingly common practice, their fraud investigators are either inept or lying.
